manifest.title: Manifest Reference
manifest.subtitle: Complete reference for the extension manifest.json file structure and all available fields.

fields.name: Unique identifier for your extension (lowercase, alphanumeric, hyphens)
fields.publicKey: Ed25519 public key (base64, generated by haex init)
fields.author: Author name displayed in the marketplace
fields.homepage: URL to your extension's homepage or repository
fields.entry: HTML entry point relative to the build output
fields.icon: Path to the extension icon (relative to extension root)
fields.displayMode: How the extension displays in haex-vault
fields.singleInstance: If true, only one instance can run at a time
fields.migrationsDir: Path (relative to extension root) to a Drizzle-style migrations directory with _journal.json and *.sql files
fields.i18n: Locale-specific overrides for name and description (key is locale code, e.g. 'de', 'en')

displayModes.auto: Automatically pick the best mode (Tauri WebviewWindow on desktop, iframe on mobile)
displayModes.window: Open in a dedicated Tauri WebviewWindow with native window controls
displayModes.iframe: Render as an embedded iframe inside haex-vault

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.intro: Declare all permissions your extension needs in the manifest. Users will review these during installation.
permissions.databaseDesc: Operations: read | readWrite | create | delete | alterDrop. Targets are the full prefixed table names of other extensions:
permissions.filesystemDesc: Operations: read | readWrite. Targets use glob patterns:
permissions.httpDesc: URL-pattern only - declare each allowed URL or domain as a target. The HTTP method is parsed from the manifest but not enforced by the runtime check today. Targets use glob patterns:
